mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for construction, which correlates with higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 60%+ of periods with HOUST YoY growth > 1.5%

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and rising oil prices (CPIAUCSL) signal supply shocks that reduce corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in > 50% of periods with INDPRO < 115 and CPIAUCSL > 2.0%

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 5%) with rising payrolls (PAYEMS) signals inflationary pressures, prompting Fed rate hikes that depress S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in > 50% of periods with UNRATE < 5% and PAYEMS > 150k

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with rising consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates robust demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in > 40% of periods with GDPC1 > 3% QoQ and RSAFS > 2% MoM

mechanism_narrative: A rising CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% signals inflation expectations that force the Fed to tighten, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in > 50% of periods with CPIAUCSL > 2.5%

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals weak supply chain capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days during recessionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in > 40% of periods with INDPRO declines > 2%

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5.5% indicates labor market deterioration, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during economic slowdowns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in > 50% of periods with UNRATE > 5.5%
